differential_diagnoses: Penetrating duodenal ulcer with duodenal-renal abscess/fistula
Foreign body ingestion with secondary duodenal-renal abscess
Retroperitoneal abscess secondary to duodenal perforation
Primary duodenal malignancy with secondary fistula and infection
Antiphospholipid syndrome presenting with renal vein thrombosis and secondary sepsis
next_steps: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) to evaluate the duodenum for perforation, foreign body, or mass
Transesophageal echocardiography to rule out endocarditis
Computed tomography (CT) scan of the abdomen with oral contrast to confirm duodenal leak and assess the fistula
Interventional radiology consultation for potential drainage of the pararenal collection
Surgical consultation for evaluation of duodenal-renal fistula
Serial blood cultures to ensure clearance of S. anginosus bacteremia
Continuation of systemic anticoagulation with close monitoring for gastrointestinal bleeding
